is the [premiere](/wiki/Premiere "Premiere") of an artistic production and its presentation and marketing to the public.

is the authorization by the owner of a completed [film](/wiki/Film "Film") to a public exhibition of the film. The exhibition may be in theaters or for home viewing. A film's **release date** and the method of release is part of the [marketing](/wiki/Marketing "Marketing") of the film. It may be a [wide](/wiki/Wide_release "Wide release") or [limited release](/wiki/Limited_theatrical_release "Limited theatrical release"). A [roadshow theatrical release](/wiki/Roadshow_theatrical_release "Roadshow theatrical release") is the practice of opening a film in a limited number of theaters in major cities for a specific period of time before the wide release of the film.

or **late release** in the [film industry](/wiki/Film_industry "Film industry") refers to the relatively late [release](/wiki/Film_release "Film release") of a film to the [public](/wiki/General_public "General public"). A release can be postponed due to the sometimes difficult transition of the production or post-production to the sales and distribution phase of the film production cycle.

usually is a creative output from an artist, available for sale or distribution; a broad term covering the many different formats music can be released in, and different forms of pieces (singles, albums, extended plays, etc.).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Premiere of an artistic production

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Art_release "Special:EditPage/Art release") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *Find sources:* ["Art release"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Art+release%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Art+release%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Art+release%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Art+release%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Art+release%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Art+release%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

An **art release** is the [premiere](/wiki/Premiere "Premiere") of an artistic production and its presentation and marketing to the public.

## Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_release&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Film")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/Led_Zeppelin_-_Celebration_Day_premiere_marquee.jpg/250px-Led_Zeppelin_-_Celebration_Day_premiere_marquee.jpg)](/wiki/File:Led_Zeppelin_-_Celebration_Day_premiere_marquee.jpg)

Film premieres can be elaborate media events, such as this 2012 exhibition of *[Celebration Day](/wiki/Celebration_Day_(film) "Celebration Day (film)")* with promotional artwork on the [Hammersmith Apollo](/wiki/Hammersmith_Apollo "Hammersmith Apollo").

A **film release** is the authorization by the owner of a completed [film](/wiki/Film "Film") to a public exhibition of the film. The exhibition may be in theaters or for home viewing. A film's **release date** and the method of release is part of the [marketing](/wiki/Marketing "Marketing") of the film. It may be a [wide](/wiki/Wide_release "Wide release") or [limited release](/wiki/Limited_theatrical_release "Limited theatrical release"). A [roadshow theatrical release](/wiki/Roadshow_theatrical_release "Roadshow theatrical release") is the practice of opening a film in a limited number of theaters in major cities for a specific period of time before the wide release of the film.

The process may involve finding a [film distributor](/wiki/Film_distributor "Film distributor"). A film's marketing may involve the film being shown at a [film festival](/wiki/Film_festival "Film festival") or [trade show](/wiki/Trade_fair "Trade fair") to attract distributor attention and, if successful, may then be released through a chosen distributor.

### Delayed release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_release&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Delayed release")]

A **delayed release** or **late release** in the [film industry](/wiki/Film_industry "Film industry") refers to the relatively late [release](/wiki/Film_release "Film release") of a film to the [public](/wiki/General_public "General public"). A release can be postponed due to the sometimes difficult transition of the production or post-production to the sales and distribution phase of the film production cycle.
Due to several factors a film release can be delayed:

1. Problems during [post-production](/wiki/Development_hell "Development hell") of an artistic nature.
2. Political problems or [censorship](/wiki/Censorship "Censorship") regarding the film.
3. Economic problems relating to limitations in the [film budget](/wiki/Film_budget "Film budget").

These problems can be resolved by overcoming artistic problems, making politically correct or commercially successful changes to the film/or relieving budgetary problems.

## Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_release&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Music")]

"Music release" redirects here. For other uses, see [Release § Music](/wiki/Release_(disambiguation)#Music "Release (disambiguation)").

In the [music industry](/wiki/Music_industry "Music industry"), a **release** usually is a creative output from an artist, available for sale or distribution; a broad term covering the many different formats music can be released in, and different forms of pieces (singles, albums, extended plays, etc.).

The word can also refer to the event at which an [album](/wiki/Album "Album") or [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") is first offered for sale in record stores. Also an **album launch**, or **single launch**. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Musical performers often [self-release](/wiki/Self-release "Self-release") (self-publish) their recordings without the involvement of an established [record label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label"). While some acts who enjoy local or small scale popularity have started their own labels in order to release their music through stores, others simply sell the music directly to customers, for example, making it available to those at their live concerts. With the growth of the Internet as a medium for publicizing and distributing music, many musical acts have sold their recordings over the Internet without a label. Unlike self-publishing a novel, which is usually done only when no other options exist, even well-established musicians will choose to self-release recordings.
Music managers are increasingly getting involved in such releases and with the advent of artist management labels which have stepped in to save the situation.[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] In Kenya, for example, most record labels only handle production, thus leading to a situation where records are marketed less. This has prompted music companies like [Grosspool Music](/wiki/Grosspool_Music "Grosspool Music") to sign independent artists and manage their branding, releases, and marketing. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_release&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Reissue](/wiki/Reissue "Reissue"), or re-release
* [Surprise release](/wiki/Surprise_release "Surprise release")
